title: Kids Play Table with Paper Roll Holder
summary: A kids activity table with a built-in dowel holder for a roll of craft paper across one end, plus a lower shelf — pull paper across the top as a rolling drawing surface.
description:
Four 2x2 legs carry the table, framed by 1x4 end and side aprons at tabletop height and a second set of 1x3 aprons lower down to carry the bottom shelf — this two-tier apron system is what gives the table real rigidity for kids leaning and climbing on it. The bottom shelf itself can be built from plywood or from a run of 1x3 boards, whichever material is on hand.

**The paper roll holder is the feature that separates this from a plain play table** — two small 1x4 blocks at one end hold a 3/4" dowel across which a roll of craft paper spins freely, letting a kid pull fresh paper across the tabletop as they draw. Mount the blocks at a height that lets the paper feed flat across the table surface.

The 1x12 top boards cap the whole assembly — leave a small gap at the paper-roll end if the roll needs to feed up and over the table edge, and confirm the fit before final attachment.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the leg-and-apron frame
steps.body: Assemble the legs with the end and side aprons at tabletop height, and the shelf aprons lower down.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the bottom shelf to the lower aprons.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the two 1x12 top boards to the frame.

steps.title: Add the paper roll holder
steps.body: Mount the two holder blocks at one end and fit the dowel across them so a paper roll spins freely.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Load the paper roll
steps.body: Fit the craft paper roll onto the dowel, feeding it across the tabletop.

unresolvedImages.alt: Kids play table with paper roll holder and shelf
